description: window.a2a_config=window.a2a_config||{};a2a_config.callbacks=[];a2a_config.overlays=[];a2a_config.templates={}; CAREER DEVELOPMENT | Careers and Counselling Centre Skip to main content University of Ghana Main navigation About Overview Staff Organizational Structure Publications Services Career Services Counselling Services Contact Us FAQs CAREER DEVELOPMENT March 19, 2026 Career development is the continuous process of managing learning, work, and personal growth to achieve professional goals. It involves improving skills, gaining experience, and making informed decisions that shape a person’s career path over time. This process includes education and training, self-assessment, setting career goals, networking, and gaining practical experience through internships or employment. Career development also involves adapting to changes in the job market, learning new technologies, and improving soft skills such as communication, leadership, and teamwork. Effective career development helps individuals: * Understand their strengths and interests * Set clear career goals * Improve professional skills and qualifications * Increase employability and promotion opportunities * Achieve long-term job satisfaction and financial stability Overall, career development empowers individuals to plan their future, grow professionally, and remain competitive in an evolving world of work. title: German-African project 2026 – 2028 on careers and startup education in the university system
organization: University of Ghana
summary: window.a2a_config=window.a2a_config||{};a2a_config.callbacks=[];a2a_config.overlays=[];a2a_config.templates={}; German-African project 2026 – 2028 on careers and startup education in the university system | Careers and Counselling Centre Skip to main content University of Ghana Main navigation About Overview Staff Organizational Structure Publications Services Career Services Counselling Services Contact Us FAQs German-African project 2026 – 2028 on careers and startup education in the university system May 6, 2026 With Erasmus + funding from the European Union, the Career Services and Startup Centres from six universities in Africa and Germany cooperate in the period from 2026 – 2028. The following universites are participating: University of Münster (Germany) University of Ghana (Ghana) Ashesi University (Ghana) United States International University-Africa (Kenya) Kepler College (Rwan
description: window.a2a_config=window.a2a_config||{};a2a_config.callbacks=[];a2a_config.overlays=[];a2a_config.templates={}; German-African project 2026 – 2028 on careers and startup education in the university system | Careers and Counselling Centre Skip to main content University of Ghana Main navigation About Overview Staff Organizational Structure Publications Services Career Services Counselling Services Contact Us FAQs German-African project 2026 – 2028 on careers and startup education in the university system May 6, 2026 With Erasmus + funding from the European Union, the Career Services and Startup Centres from six universities in Africa and Germany cooperate in the period from 2026 – 2028. The following universites are participating: University of Münster (Germany) University of Ghana (Ghana) Ashesi University (Ghana) United States International University-Africa (Kenya) Kepler College (Rwanda) American University in Cairo (Egypt). The primary aim of the project is to enable students at the participating universities to gain mutual insights into the academic labour markets, into startup founding, into culture and the social situation of the countries represented and into everyday life in Africa and Europe. They will gain intercultural experience, can prepare themselves for a globalised working life and can explore individual, international career prospects. The project aims to build expertise and knowledge among students and university staff at the participating institutions, and to develop a current and realistic picture of developments and prospects in Europe and Africa. These processes will be facilitated through the following formats and activities: Exchange of expertise among university staff on the topics of careers education and startup founding Online seminars and o
source: University of Ghana

description: window.a2a_config=window.a2a_config||{};a2a_config.callbacks=[];a2a_config.overlays=[];a2a_config.templates={}; LEADERSHIP ACADEMY | Careers and Counselling Centre Skip to main content University of Ghana Main navigation About Overview Staff Organizational Structure Publications Services Career Services Counselling Services Contact Us FAQs LEADERSHIP ACADEMY March 19, 2026 A Leadership Academy is a structured training program designed to develop individuals’ leadership abilities, personal effectiveness, and professional skills. It prepares participants to take on leadership roles in schools, organizations, businesses, and communities. Leadership academies focus on building core competencies such as communication, teamwork, decision-making, problem-solving, emotional intelligence, and ethical leadership. Participants often engage in workshops, mentorship programs, simulations, group projects, and real-world leadership activities that strengthen confidence and responsibility. Many well-known institutions run leadership academies, such as Harvard Kennedy School, United Nations Leadership Academy, and corporate programs like Google Leadership Development Programs. Overall, a leadership academy helps individuals: * Discover their leadership potential * Develop practical leadership skills * Build professional networks * Gain mentorship and guidance * Prepare for future leadership responsibilities It is valuable for students, young professionals, managers, entrepreneurs, and anyone who wants to positively influence others and drive meaningful change. description: window.a2a_config=window.a2a_config||{};a2a_config.callbacks=[];a2a_config.overlays=[];a2a_config.templates={}; UG SAYS NO TO DRUGS 🚫💊 | Careers and Counselling Centre Skip to main content University of Ghana Main navigation About Overview Staff Organizational Structure Publications Services Career Services Counselling Services Contact Us FAQs UG SAYS NO TO DRUGS 🚫💊 March 12, 2026 UNIVERSITY OF GHANA – UG SAYS NO TO DRUGS 🚫💊 The “UG Says No to Drugs” campaign is a student-focused awareness initiative aimed at promoting a healthy, safe, and drug-free environment within the University of Ghana community. The campaign seeks to educate students about the dangers of drug abuse, encourage responsible decision-making, and empower the university community to stand firmly against substance misuse. Drug abuse remains a growing concern among young people worldwide, and universities are not exempt from its effects. The misuse of drugs can negatively impact students’ academic performance, mental health, physical well-being, and future opportunities. Through this campaign, the University of Ghana reaffirms its commitment to protecting students and promoting a culture of wellness and responsibility on campus. summary: المعهد العالي للتقنيات الهندسية .container { width: 100%; margin: 20px auto; padding: 15px; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0px 4px 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1); } a { text-decoration: none !important; } a:hover { text-decoration: none !important; } .section-title { text-align: center; margin-bottom: 20px; } .section-title h1 { font-size: 24px; color: #1e3a8a; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 10px; } .section-title img { margin-top: 15px; border-radius: 8px; max-width: 100%; height: auto; } p { font-size: 16px; color: #444; line-height: 1.6; text-align: justify; margin-bottom: 15px; } .departments ul { list-style: none; padding: 0; } .departments li { font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .departments li::before { content: "• "; color: #007bff; margin-left: 5px; } .video-section { display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; background: #f0f0f0; padding: 20px 0; } .video-co

title: Se rendre en consultation
organization: Centre Hospitalo-Universitaire Mohammed VI d'Oujda
summary: Se rendre en consultation | CHU Mohammed VI Oujda (SAMU05) 141 STANDARD +2125369000 CHU OUJDA STATUS JURIDIQUE .cls-1{fill:#010101;} organigramme ORGANIGRAMME .cls-1{fill:#010101;} missions du CHU MISSION DE CHU REGLEMENTATIONS Fermer PATIENTS & PROCHES .cls-1{fill:#010101;}.cls-2{fill:#fff;} se rendre en consultation SE RENDRE EN CONSULTATION S'HOSPITALISER .cls-1{fill:#010101;} les urgences LES URGENCES .cls-1{fill:#010101;} visiter un patient VISITER UN PATIENT .cls-1{fill:#010101;} Charte du patient LA CHARTE DU PATIENT Fermer STAGES & RECRUTEMENTS OFFRE DE STAGE Candidature spantanée .cls-1{fill:#010101;} RECRUTEMENT Fermer Soins & Spécialités Hôpital des spécialités Hôpital Mère et enfant Centre d'oncologie Hassan II Hôpital de la santé mentale Fermer NOS COMITÉS Accueil Patients et proches Se Rendre En Consultation SE RENDRE EN CONSULTATION Prendre un rendez-vous La première étape
description: Se rendre en consultation | CHU Mohammed VI Oujda (SAMU05) 141 STANDARD +2125369000 CHU OUJDA STATUS JURIDIQUE .cls-1{fill:#010101;} organigramme ORGANIGRAMME .cls-1{fill:#010101;} missions du CHU MISSION DE CHU REGLEMENTATIONS Fermer PATIENTS & PROCHES .cls-1{fill:#010101;}.cls-2{fill:#fff;} se rendre en consultation SE RENDRE EN CONSULTATION S'HOSPITALISER .cls-1{fill:#010101;} les urgences LES URGENCES .cls-1{fill:#010101;} visiter un patient VISITER UN PATIENT .cls-1{fill:#010101;} Charte du patient LA CHARTE DU PATIENT Fermer STAGES & RECRUTEMENTS OFFRE DE STAGE Candidature spantanée .cls-1{fill:#010101;} RECRUTEMENT Fermer Soins & Spécialités Hôpital des spécialités Hôpital Mère et enfant Centre d'oncologie Hassan II Hôpital de la santé mentale Fermer NOS COMITÉS Accueil Patients et proches Se Rendre En Consultation SE RENDRE EN CONSULTATION Prendre un rendez-vous La première étape pour bénéficier d'une consultation est de se présenter à l'accueil du Centre de Consultations pour prendre un rendez-vous. Les pièces à fournir sont comme suit : Pour les patients bénéficiant du régime d'assistance médicale RAMED : Copie de la CIN Copie de la carte RAMED La fiche de liaison ou bien l'ordonnance fournie par le Centre Hospitalier Régional (El farabi), en y indiquant le service ou la spécialité à consulter et les renseignements cliniques du patient. Pour les patients mutualistes : Copie de la CIN Copie de la carte de la mutuelle L'ordonnance fournie par un centre hospitalier, une clinique ou un médecin, en y indiquant le service ou la spécialité à consulter et les renseignements cliniques du patient. Pour les patients payants : Copie de la CIN L'ordonnance fournie par un centre hospitalier, une clinique ou un médecin, en y indiquant le service ou la spécialité à consulter
source: Centre Hospitalo-Universitaire Mohammed VI d'Oujda
